stimulus: The Niger-Congo language family encompasses Yoruba, Swahili, and roughly 1,500 other languages across sub-Saharan Africa, making it one of the most extensive language families in the world and of central importance to ______ of the University of Cape Town.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice D is correct. No punctuation is needed to set off the proper noun "Lutz Marten" from the title "linguist," because the name is essential to identify which linguist is meant.

Choice A is incorrect because a comma after the name treats it as if more nonessential information followed, which is not the case. Choice B is incorrect because enclosing the name in commas treats it as nonessential, suggesting it could be removed without affecting the sentence’s meaning. Choice C is incorrect because a colon between "linguist" and the name disrupts the sentence’s flow, suggesting an introductory list rather than direct identification.
